name: Contact address (branch address landing)
pageTitle: Contact address for consignment - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: Complete notification
heading: Contact address for consignment
bodyCopy: This is the contact address of the person responsible for the consignment from when it enters Great Britain until authorities complete their checks.
If the correct address is not shown, add a new branch address, then return to this page.

journeyPosition.followedBy: Branch Address Creation Page (via 'add a new branch address' link) then Review Notification Page
journeyPosition.evidence: ipaffs-qa-automation/tests/accessibility/ched-p-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:245-276 — Nominated Contacts → 'Contact Address Page' → add new branch address → Branch Address Creation/Confirmation → Review Notification; same order in workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:523-533

structure.text: This is the contact address of the person responsible for the consignment from when it enters Great Britain until authorities complete their checks.
If the correct address is not shown, add a new branch address, then return to this page.
Address radio list (16 options observed — account/address-book driven)

govukComponents.usage: Section caption 'Complete notification' above the H1
Groups the address radios under the legend 'Select an address'
Single-select list of saved addresses (organisation main address + address-book branch addresses); user picks the contact address for the consignment
govukComponents.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 154 — 16 radio inputs with name=branch-address-select. NOTE: the QA suite never drives this radio group — no page-object selection method exists; every workflow bypasses it by clicking 'add a new branch address'. Radio corroboration is trace-only.

govukComponents.usage: Intro paragraph under the H1 and the 'add a new branch address' helper paragraph above the radios
'add a new branch address' inline link (id=add-branch-address) that navigates to the new-branch-address form
govukComponents.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 154 — href /notification/vnet/protected/notifications/DRAFT.GB.2026.1525979/traders/organisation branch address/new. Corroborated by page object: ContactAddressPage.ts:6-8 getByRole('link', { name: 'add a new branch address' }).

govukComponents.usage: 'Save and return to hub' and 'Save and continue' — dual primary submit buttons
govukComponents.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 154. 'Save and continue' corroborated by page object ContactAddressPage.ts:10-12 getByRole('button', { name: 'Save and continue' }); 'Save and return to hub' is trace-only (never exercised by the suite).

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A bespoke hook class on the address radio group / container (radio inputs share name=branch-address-select). Serves as a CSS/JS selector hook, not a Design System component.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Only non-govuk class on the page. Harmless namespacing hook rather than a bespoke widget — the underlying control is a standard govuk Radios group.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: None needed — the radios are already the standard GOV.UK Radios component; drop the hook class or replace with a data attribute in the rebuild.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 154 — nonstd class inventory returned only 'branch-address-select'

fields.name: branch-address-select
fields.label: Select an address
fields.hint: If the correct address is not shown, add a new branch address, then return to this page.
options: 34 GREYSTONE PARK, CREWE, CHESHIRE EAST, CW1 2AL United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland
43 West Hague Extension Main street Castlereagh Belfast 78145 Switzerland
43 East Hague Extension Delectus sit odio p Laborum Odio tempor Quas occaecat ut ear 30055
fields.conditionalOn: always — address list is account/address-book driven reference data (org main address at value "0" is pre-checked; remaining options are saved branch addresses keyed by UUID)
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 154 — 16 radio inputs; first value=0 id=0-branch-address checked (34 GREYSTONE PARK...), remainder UUID-valued; only 3 distinct address strings recur across 16 seeded test radios. Page-object corroboration is ABSENT: ContactAddressPage.ts exposes only linkAddNewBranchAddress + btnSaveAndContinue — no method selects an existing radio, and every workflow (ched-p-workflows.ts:526, 798, 1003) plus both accessibility specs click 'add a new branch address' instead. So the required-ness / server enforcement of this selection is not testable from the QA suite; DOM required=false is the only signal and no error copy was ever asserted.

validationMessages.trigger: Submitting 'Save and continue' with no address selected (expected — a selection is functionally required to set the consignment contact address)
validationMessages.evidence: No error state observed — this trace had zero errors and the default option was pre-checked; the required-selection error copy was never rendered in the mined trace. The QA suite adds no evidence: grep of tests/ finds no expect() / assertion on this page at all (only accessibility axe scans and navigation), so there is no inferred error string to promote. Remains a genuine gap for a human.

accessibility.evidence: ipaffs-qa-automation/tests/accessibility/ched-p-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:249 checkPageAccessibility(pages.page, 'Contact Address Page') — the page is included in the CHED-P axe sweep and is expected to pass with no violations; same in ched-d-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:171 context.

openQuestions: The radio inputs report required=false at the DOM level, yet a contact address selection is functionally mandatory (the org main address is pre-checked so a value is always present). Confirm whether IPAFFS enforces selection server-side and what the validation message reads — never observed because no error trace exists for this page and no QA test asserts on it.

The address options are user/address-book reference data (16 seeded radios collapse to 3 distinct address strings in the test account). The new app will source these from a real address/address-book service rather than hardcode them — count and content are account-specific, not a fixed enum.

URL is inferred from the returnUrl hidden field (…/DRAFT.GB.2026.1525979/organisation-branch-address) and the 'add a new branch address' href; the page's own GET route was not directly captured as a Navigate action in this trace.

Two submit buttons ('Save and return to hub' vs 'Save and continue') both post the selection — confirm the distinct routing/behaviour of each in the rebuild. Only 'Save and continue' is exercised by the QA suite; 'Save and return to hub' and 'Cancel and return to hub' are trace-only.

COVERAGE GAP: the QA automation never selects an existing address on this page — every CHED-P/CHED-P-fish/accessibility flow immediately clicks 'add a new branch address', creates one, and returns. So the primary control of the page (choosing from the saved-address radio list) has NO test corroboration; it is entirely trace-evidenced. The rebuild should add coverage for the select-existing-address path.

IUU RELEVANCE CONFIRMED: the CHED-P fish (catch-certificate) flow — createChedPFishNotificationUpToReview, ched-p-workflows.ts:840,1003 — walks this same Contact Address page identically (add new branch address → creation → confirmation → save and continue), so the page is in scope for the standalone IUU journey.
